hero.title: About Me
hero.subtitle: I’m a Frontend Developer and Product Designer who builds modern web applications with a strong focus on usability, performance, and clean architecture.
hero.description: With a background in both design and engineering, I bridge the gap between visual creativity and technical execution. My goal is simple: create digital products that are beautiful, intuitive, and scalable.
hero.works: I work with companies and startups around the world to transform ideas into high-quality web experiences — also helping women launch tech careers through Tech Beach Mama.

background.title: My Background
background.description: My path into tech started from the design side.
background.description2: I began my career working with graphic design and user experience, helping brands communicate visually and craft better digital products. Over time, I became increasingly interested in the technical side of building those experiences.
background.description3: That curiosity led me to transition into frontend development, where I could combine design thinking with engineering.
background.description4: Today, I specialize in building modern web applications with React and Next.js, focusing on performance, scalability, and exceptional user experience.
background.description5: With experience working in professional development teams and delivering production-ready applications, I understand what it takes to build software that is not only visually polished but also maintainable and reliable.

whatIDo.title: What I Do
whatIDo.description: I help companies design and build modern digital products.
whatIDo.workFocus: My work focuses on three areas:
whatIDo.fastBuilding: Building fast, scalable, and maintainable web applications using modern technologies.
whatIDo.intuitiveDesign: Designing interfaces that are intuitive, elegant, and user-focused.
whatIDo.designDrivenDevelopmentDescription: Combining design and development to create products where form and function work together seamlessly.

approach.title: My Approach
approach.description: I believe great digital products are created when design and engineering work together from the beginning.
approach.iFocusOn: Instead of simply implementing designs, I focus on solving real product problems and building experiences that users actually enjoy using.
approach.alsoPassionateAbout: I’m also passionate about building reusable UI systems and scalable frontend architectures.

quote.description: Ideas bloom where the mind wanders, not just where the screen glows.

contact.description: If you’re looking for a developer who understands both product design and frontend engineering, I’d love to hear about your project.

experience.title: My long experience counts on
experience.scalableWebApplications: years of Building scalable web applications

techBeachMama.title: Beyond client work
techBeachMama.description: I’m also building Tech Beach Mama — a platform where I help women, especially single mothers, transition into tech and build independent, flexible careers.
techBeachMama.description2: It’s where I share my journey, resources, and practical guidance for creating a life with more freedom.
techBeachMama.linkText: → Learn more about Tech Beach Mama

hero.title: My services
hero.subtitle: Frontend Development & UI Design. I help businesses build fast, scalable, and beautifully designed web experiences.
hero.description: I help you bring your ideas to life with a clean design and reliable frontend development — creating websites and applications that are both beautiful and built to perform.

cards.title: What I can help you with
cards.description: I combine frontend development, UI design, and product thinking to create digital experiences that are both beautiful and built to perform.

process.title: My process
process.description: My process is simple and straightforward. I follow a step-by-step approach to build your project.

steps.discoveryDescription: Understanding your goals, audience, and vision to define the right direction.
steps.designDescription: Creating intuitive layouts and a visual identity that reflects your brand.
steps.developmentDescription: Building a fast, scalable, and reliable product using modern technologies.
steps.launchDescription: Testing, refining, and launching your project with performance in mind.

quote.description: I combine engineering precision with design intuition — so your product not only works, but feels right.

testimonials.title: What my clients say about me
testimonials.subtitle: I have worked with a variety of clients, from startups to large corporations. Here is what they have to say about me.

list.name: Fernando Rueda Oliva
list.position: CTO Quantum engineer and Solutions Architect
list.testimonial: I’ve had the pleasure of working alongside Asa at Hexad for several years, and I can confidently say she is one of the most adaptable, positive, and inspiring colleagues I’ve ever met. Asa’s attitude is truly exceptional—no matter how complex or unfamiliar the challenge, she always greets it with a smile and an open mind. Her journey from a talented UI designer to a skilled frontend developer is a testament to her courage and determination to grow. Not only did she master new technical skills, but she also stepped up to take on the responsibilities of a Project Manager, seamlessly adapting to each new role with enthusiasm and professionalism. What sets Asa apart is her flexibility and her fearless approach to change. She never shies away from stepping outside her comfort zone, and her positive energy uplifts everyone around her. Asa’s ability to wear multiple hats, learn quickly, and maintain a can-do attitude has been invaluable to our team. Thank you, Asa, for your dedication, your positivity, and for all the years you’ve contributed to Hexad. It’s been a privilege to work with you, and I look forward to seeing all the great things you’ll accomplish in the future!

list.name: Catalina Golia
list.testimonial: It was a pleasure working with her on the development of my website. She understood exactly what I needed from the very beginning, worked with great speed and dedication, and provided very valuable advice that significantly improved the final result. I would highlight both her strong technical expertise and her close, professional approach, which made the entire process smooth and efficient. I highly recommend her as an expert web developer. Thank you Asa!

pricing.designAndDevelopment: Design + development in one place — no need to hire multiple people.

hero.title: Selected Work
hero.subtitle: A selection of projects where I’ve combined frontend development, UI design, and product thinking to build modern, high-performing web experiences.
hero.description: Each project reflects my approach to creating digital products that are not only visually refined, but also scalable, intuitive, and built with performance in mind.

techBeachMama.title: Tech Beach Mama
techBeachMama.description: A high-converting personal brand website and growing digital business — designed and built in Next.js, Tailwind CSS, and Shadcn UI to inspire women to start frontend careers and achieve financial freedom through tech by the beach.

hero.subtitle: Building a personal brand, a newsletter funnel, and a digital product business from scratch
hero.description: Tech Beach Mama is not just a website — it is a growing platform and a personal mission. Launched after leaving employment to go independent, it is designed to inspire women to enter frontend development and build careers that work for their lives. The site serves as the digital home for a brand built on Instagram, a newsletter funnel, and a growing suite of digital products — all built and owned by me, and continuing to evolve.

step1.label: The Vision
step1.title: A platform born from a career decision
textItems: Tech Beach Mama started after I left my job to go fully independent. The motivation was straightforward: I had spent years building skills in frontend development and product design, and I wanted to help other women — particularly mothers — do the same, without having to start from zero the way I did.

The platform is built around a simple funnel: attract women through Instagram who are curious about tech careers, bring them to the website to offer real value through free and paid resources, and grow a community of women building financial independence through web development, UI/UX design, and project management.

This is a long-term project. Unlike client work, which has a defined end point, Tech Beach Mama is designed to grow over years — new content, new products, and an audience that expands as the brand does. That shaped every technical and design decision from the start.

challenges.title: Building a personal brand platform from scratch comes with its own set of constraints.
rows: Designed, built, and maintained entirely solo alongside freelance client work
Needed to function as a real conversion funnel, not just a portfolio piece
Architecture must scale as the product offering grows over time
Visual identity and tone had to feel distinct and personal, not generic

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Clean, modern, and built to convert
textItems: The design was created in Figma with conversion as a first-class concern alongside aesthetics. The visual language needed to feel warm, aspirational, and credible — communicating that this is a serious resource built by someone who has actually done what she is teaching. Clean layout, intentional typography, and a colour palette that feels modern without being cold. Every section is structured to guide the visitor toward the newsletter signup or a digital product.
step2.alt: Tech Beach Mama design concept — Figma
step2.caption: Design concept — Figma

step3.label: Stack & Architecture
step3.title: Built for performance, flexibility, and long-term growth
textItems: The stack was chosen specifically for what this platform needs to do over the long term: fast performance, easy content updates, and the flexibility to add new product types, pages, and integrations without rebuilding from scratch.

Next.js handles server-side rendering and routing, Tailwind CSS gives full design control without a bloated stylesheet, and Shadcn UI provides accessible, composable components that keep the codebase clean as the project grows. The repository is private — this is a live commercial project, not just a demonstration piece.

solution.title: The technical stack was selected to support a growing digital product business.
rows: Next.js for performance, SEO, and scalable routing
Tailwind CSS for design control and rapid iteration
Shadcn UI for accessible, composable component architecture
Figma-first design process before implementation
Newsletter funnel integration for audience building
Architecture ready for expanding digital product catalogue

step4.label: The Funnel
step4.title: From Instagram to email list to digital products
textItems: The platform is built around a deliberate conversion path. Visitors arrive primarily from Instagram, where the brand publishes content about frontend careers, freelancing, and the lifestyle that goes with them. The website captures that interest with a free resource — an eBook on how to start a frontend career — that feeds into an automated email sequence.

The current product offering includes the free lead magnet eBook and a paid eBook on freelancing. The roadmap includes further digital products, courses, and resources as the audience grows. Every page on the site is designed with this funnel in mind — not as an afterthought, but as the structural logic the entire site is built around.
step4.alt: Tech Beach Mama live site — homepage and newsletter funnel
step4.caption: The live platform — conversion-focused layout, newsletter funnel, and digital products

step5.label: Content & Products
step5.title: Real resources for women entering tech
textItems: The content on Tech Beach Mama is drawn directly from my own experience: learning design before code, rebuilding professionally, going independent, and growing income over time. That personal narrative runs through everything — the Instagram content, the eBooks, and the site copy.

Current offerings: a free eBook on starting a frontend career as part of the lead magnet funnel, and a paid eBook on freelancing. The platform is intentionally built to expand — new digital products, resources, and eventually community features are part of the long-term roadmap.

offerings.title: What the platform currently offers:
rows: Free eBook — Remote, Respected & Rich: A Strategic Frontend Roadmap for Modern Mothers
Paid eBook on freelancing and building an independent career in tech
Automated email sequence via MailerLite for nurturing new subscribers
Active Instagram channel driving organic traffic to the funnel
Roadmap includes courses, templates, and community features

step5.alt: Tech Beach Mama digital product section featuring frontend career eBook and freelancing guide for women transitioning into tech
step5.caption: Digital product ecosystem — free and paid resources designed to help women transition into tech and build independent careers

step6.label: Performance & SEO
step6.title: Built to be found and fast to load
textItems: Performance and SEO were built in from the start, not added afterwards. Next.js server-side rendering ensures pages are indexable and fast, Tailwind keeps the CSS footprint minimal, and the component architecture avoids the kind of bloat that slows down content-heavy sites over time.

For a platform that depends on organic discovery and social traffic converting into newsletter subscribers, load speed and Core Web Vitals directly affect conversion rates — not just search rankings. That connection between technical quality and business outcome shaped every implementation decision.
step6.alt: Tech Beach Mama Lighthouse performance scores
step6.caption: Performance · Lighthouse scores

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A live platform, a growing audience, and a long-term project
textItems: Tech Beach Mama is the project I am most personally invested in. It is simultaneously a demonstration of what I can build technically, a real business with real products and a growing audience, and a creative outlet that sits outside client work.
It is also the project that prompted an interviewer to call it a great personal project — which, for something built solo alongside everything else, felt like the right kind of validation.

results.title: What the project demonstrates:
rows: End-to-end ownership — design, development, content, and business strategy
Conversion-focused design applied to a real funnel with real products
Production-grade Next.js architecture built for long-term scalability
Active audience building via Instagram and an automated email funnel
A platform that continues to grow — this case study will keep evolving

finalCta.title: Want a website that works as hard as you do?

sculptedGoddess.title: Sculpted Goddess
sculptedGoddess.description: A nutrition and fitness web application — originally built as a CS50 final project in Python and Flask, later rebuilt from the ground up in Next.js and TypeScript to demonstrate modern frontend architecture, performance, and long-term maintainability.

hero.subtitle: From a CS50 final project to a modern, full-stack fitness platform
hero.description: Sculpted Goddess started as a data-driven nutrition and fitness web application, built as the final project for Harvard's CS50x Introduction to Computer Science course. Years later, it became a vehicle for something more: a full architectural rebuild in Next.js and TypeScript, demonstrating how legacy applications can be modernised without losing their core purpose. The project tracks personal fitness data, calculates macronutrient needs, and lets users search and save recipes — all rebuilt on a foundation designed to last.

step1.label: The Origin
step1.title: A CS50 final project built in Python and Flask
textItems: Sculpted Goddess was created as the final project for Harvard's CS50x Introduction to Computer Science course — a nutrition and fitness tool built to help women set up personalised meal plans and work toward their fitness goals.

The original version was built with Python, Flask, SQL, JavaScript, HTML, and SCSS. It connected to the Edamam Recipe Search API and handled user authentication, a macronutrient calculator, and recipe search and saving — all server-side rendered. The design was created in Adobe XD before a single line of code was written.

The application worked, but over time it became increasingly difficult to maintain and extend. The Python knowledge faded, the API integration aged, and the architecture wasn't built to scale. That gap between the original idea and what the technology could actually support became the motivation for a complete rebuild.

challenges.title: The original setup had served its purpose, but came with real long-term limitations.
rows: Python/Flask stack no longer actively maintained
Edamam API integration needed replacing with MealDB
Architecture not designed to scale or extend
Original Adobe XD design file lost — rebuild required recreating from scratch

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Starting with design — then and now
textItems: The original Sculpted Goddess was designed in Adobe XD before any code was written — a deliberate, design-first approach that carried into the rebuilt version. The visual language needed to feel strong and intentional: a platform for women who are serious about nutrition and fitness, not a generic wellness app. Clean structure, clear hierarchy, and a confident aesthetic that communicates trust and precision.
step2.alt: Sculpted Goddess original Adobe XD design concept
step2.caption: Original design concept — Adobe XD

step3.label: Rebuild & Architecture
step3.title: Rebuilding the platform on a modern stack
textItems: The rebuild was not a cosmetic update — it was a complete re-engineering of the application from the ground up. The goal was to keep the core product logic intact while replacing the technical foundation with something modern, maintainable, and scalable.

The new stack uses Next.js with TypeScript for server-side rendering and type safety, SCSS for styling, and libSQL for the database layer. The Edamam API integration was replaced with MealDB, a free and actively maintained recipe API. User authentication, recipe saving, and the macronutrient calculator were all rebuilt with the same feature scope — but with significantly cleaner architecture underneath.

solution.title: The rebuild focused on architectural quality and long-term maintainability.
rows: Migrated from Python/Flask to Next.js with TypeScript
Replaced deprecated Edamam API with MealDB integration
Rebuilt database layer using libSQL
Preserved all core features: recipe search, saving, macro calculator, contact form
Added full TypeScript coverage for type safety and developer confidence
Redesigned the user experience for clarity and mobile responsiveness

step4.label: Core Features
step4.title: What the platform does
textItems: Sculpted Goddess is built around four main features: a recipe search powered by the MealDB API with individual nutritional data per recipe, a personal recipe library where logged-in users can save and revisit their favourites, a macronutrient and BMI calculator that takes personal data and fitness goals as input, and a contact form for direct client enquiries.

The platform is open to browse for anyone, but signing up and logging in unlocks the full feature set — recipe saving, personal data, and macro tracking. This structure keeps the barrier to entry low while creating a meaningful reason to register.
step4.alt: Sculpted Goddess live — recipe search and nutrition features
step4.caption: The rebuilt platform live — recipe search, nutrition data, and personal macro tracking

step5.label: Technical Evolution
step5.title: From Flask to Next.js — what changed and why
textItems: The technical improvements in the rebuild are not simply about switching frameworks. They reflect a fundamentally different approach to how the application is structured, typed, and maintained over time.

Note: Python and Flask are excellent tools for many use cases. The decision to rebuild in Next.js was driven by the specific goals of this project: demonstrating modern frontend architecture, improving long-term maintainability, and aligning the codebase with the stack I use for client work.

before.title: Original (Python / Flask)
rows: Server-side rendering with Flask
SQL database, no type safety
Edamam Recipe API (now replaced)
Plain JavaScript, no TypeScript
Limited architecture scalability

after.title: Rebuilt (Next.js / TypeScript)
rows: Next.js with server-side rendering
libSQL database with TypeScript throughout
MealDB API — free, maintained, flexible
Full TypeScript coverage
Clean, scalable frontend architecture

step5.alt: Lighthouse performance scores — Python/Flask before vs Next.js after
step5.caption: Performance · Python/Flask vs Next.js (Lighthouse)

step6.label: Design System
step6.title: A structured SCSS design system built for consistency across the application
textItems: Sculpted Goddess is styled through a structured SCSS architecture: colors.scss, fontSizes.scss, and breakPoints.scss are imported into a central constants.module.scss that exports all design tokens directly to JavaScript components. Spacing, typography, breakpoints, and colour values are defined once and consumed throughout the application — no magic numbers, no repeated hex codes.

The typography system uses two distinct typefaces with clear roles: Cinzel (serif) for h1 headings — uppercase, high-impact, and fitting for the brand — and Open Sans for all body text, labels, and navigation. Font sizes are defined across three explicit breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop) via CSS custom properties set in globals.scss, giving every text element a consistent, predictable scale across devices.
step6.alt: Sculpted Goddess design system — colour palette and responsive typography scale
step6.caption: Design system · SCSS colour tokens and responsive type scale

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A personal project that became a professional case study
textItems: Sculpted Goddess grew from a course assignment into a real demonstration of how I approach complex problems: starting with design, building with intention, and being willing to rebuild something entirely when the foundation no longer serves the product.

This project reflects exactly the kind of work I do for clients — modernising legacy applications, migrating to scalable architectures, and rebuilding products without losing the core business logic that made them valuable in the first place.

results.title: The rebuild resulted in:
rows: A fully modernised stack built on Next.js and TypeScript
Improved performance and Core Web Vitals
A clean, maintainable codebase built to extend
Active, maintained API integration via MealDB
A demonstrable example of modern frontend architecture for client conversations

finalCta.title: Working with a legacy codebase that needs modernising?

cgConsulting.title: CG Consulting
cgConsulting.description: A complete redesign and platform migration for a Spanish HR consultancy — from a fragile WordPress setup to a fast, bilingual Next.js web app with Sanity CMS, built to last without ongoing maintenance costs.

hero.subtitle: From WordPress to a scalable, high-performance web architecture
hero.description: A complete redesign and rebuild of a consulting website, focused on performance, maintainability, and a clean, professional user experience. Many small businesses rely on WordPress setups that become fragile over time. This project shows how migrating to a modern stack can reduce long-term risk while improving performance and flexibility.

step1.label: The Problem
step1.title: A WordPress site held together by fragile plugins
textItems: CG Consulting is a boutique HR services firm helping international companies find talent in Spain.

The existing WordPress website had served the business for years but had become increasingly fragile due to plugin dependencies and lack of ongoing maintenance. They needed a stable, professional digital presence that could be managed without relying on a developer for day-to-day updates — in both Spanish and English.
The goal of this project was not only to redesign the website, but to rebuild it on a modern, scalable architecture that removes technical risk and supports future growth.

challenges.title: The main challenge was not the platform itself, but the long-term sustainability of the existing setup.
rows: Plugin conflicts risking content loss
No budget for ongoing maintenance
Slow page loads, no performance control
Client dependent on developer for updates

step2.label: Design Concept
step2.title: Starting with design, not code
textItems: The redesign centered on clarity and credibility — the two things an independent HR consultant needs to communicate immediately. I focused on a clean visual hierarchy, structured content presentation, and a layout that feels professional without being cold. Subtle animations and transitions, built with Framer Motion, add movement and polish to the experience without pulling attention away from the content. The site is fully responsive, designed to feel equally trustworthy on mobile and desktop.
step2.caption: Redesign concept — Figma

step3.label: Migration & Architecture
step3.title: Moving from WordPress to a modern stack
textItems: I proposed a full platform migration from WordPress to a modern Next.js + Sanity CMS stack. The goal wasn't just a technical upgrade — it was to give the client genuine ownership of their content and confidence that their site wouldn't break without a developer on call.
Since CG Consulting works with international companies to source talent in Spain, the site needed to speak to two audiences. Full bilingual support in Spanish and English was built in from the start.

solution.title: I rebuilt the website using a modern, lightweight architecture focused on simplicity and control.
rows: Removed dependency on plugins
Introduced structured content via Sanity CMS
Built a custom frontend for full control over performance and design
Implemented a responsive, mobile-first layout
Added subtle animations to enhance the user experience

step4.label: The Live Site
step4.title: Design in the browser — desktop & mobile
textItems: The redesigned site live — clean layout, professional credibility, and a fully responsive experience across all devices.
step4.caption: The redesigned site live — clean layout, professional credibility, and a fully responsive experience across all devices.

step5.label: Performance
step5.title: From slow to fast — measurable results
textItems: Performance and SEO improvements are not tied to a specific framework, but to how the application is built. In this project, the improvements came from simplifying the architecture, reducing dependencies, and optimizing the implementation.

Note: WordPress can absolutely deliver excellent performance and SEO when built with a clean architecture and minimal dependencies. In this case, the chosen approach prioritized long-term stability, performance control, and a simplified maintenance experience for the client.

before.title: Before
rows: Plugin-heavy WordPress setup
Recurring maintenance issues
Slower performance
Limited flexibility

after.title: After
rows: Lightweight custom architecture
Stable and predictable setup
Optimized performance
Full control over frontend and content

step5.alt: Lighthouse performance scores — WordPress before vs Next.js after
step5.caption: Performance · WordPress vs Next.js (Lighthouse)

step6.label: Client Handoff
step6.title: CG Consulting can manage their own content — no developer needed
textItems: Sanity CMS was configured to give the client a clean, simple editing interface. They can update their services, copy, and contact info without touching code — no developer required after launch.
step6.alt: Sanity Studio content management interface for CG Consulting
step6.caption: Content management · Sanity Studio

step7.label: The Outcome
step7.title: A stable, bilingual web app built to last
textItems: The client went from worrying about plugin conflicts to having full control over her content through a clean CMS interface. The new site is significantly faster, far more stable, and built on a foundation that can scale as her business does — without depending on a developer for day-to-day updates.
This project reflects how I approach client work: solving a real business problem, not just building a website.

results.title: The new setup resulted in:
rows: Faster load times
Improved Core Web Vitals
Cleaner, more maintainable codebase
Easier content management
Reduced long-term maintenance needs

finalCta.title: Looking to modernize your website or improve performance?
